title: The 7 Most Undervalued Blue-Chip Stocks to Buy Now: June 2023
text: Looking for undervalued blue-chip stocks to buy? This is a great time to be doing so.

title: Chicago executive and philanthropist Jim Crown killed on 70th birthday in crash at Colorado automobile race track
text: WOODY CREEK, Colo. (AP) — Jim Crown, an executive and philanthropist who recently announced an effort to rally other business leaders to help fight violent crime in Chicago, died Sunday in a car crash on a racing track in Colorado.

title: General Dynamics (GD) Wins $712M Deal for Stryker DVHA1 Vehicle
text: General Dynamics' (GD) business unit clinches a $712.3-million deal to provide 300 Stryker DVHA1 combat vehicles to the U.S. Army.

title: James Crown, Chicago businessman and JPMorgan board member, dies at 70 after car crash at Colorado racetrack
text: James Crown, a wealthy Chicago businessman who served on the boards of JPMorgan Chase & Co. JPM, +0.24% and General Dynamics Corp. GD, -0.14%, died Sunday on his 70th birthday in a car crash at a Colorado racetrack, according to reports. In a statement, the Pitkin County Coroner's Office, which is investigating the incident, said that Crown was “involved in a single-vehicle accident” at the Aspen Motorsports Park in Woody Creek, Colo.

title: General Dynamics, American Rheinmetall chosen to compete to win potential $45 bln US Army vehicle contract
text: The U.S. Army has selected General Dynamics and American Rheinmetall to compete to replace the Bradley fighting vehicle, in a deal that could be worth more than $45 billion to the winner.

title: Defense Stocks Seem Timely to Me Now
text: Vladimir Putin invades Ukraine and sabre-rattles about nuclear weapons. Xi Jinping says he wants his military to be ready to invade Taiwan by 2027.

title: General Dynamics (GD) Rides on Backlog & Product Development
text: General Dynamics (GD) is expected to benefit from strategic investment plans, product innovations and rising backlog.

title: Dividend Income Update May 2023
text: Without rehashing the wild ride we experienced in the market so far in 2023, I could find comfort in one thing, my dividends. Hitting the elusive four-digit mark in May has been a welcome sign for me on my dividend growth journey. In May 2022 I brought in $862.43 and this May I have seen a year over year increase of 23.8%.

title: General Dynamics: A Cheap Dividend Aristocrat With Huge Total Return Potential
text: General Dynamics is a proven compounder with a unique place in the defense industrial complex, making it a safe long-term investment for dividend investors. The company has consistently delivered returns and is an established dividend aristocrat. Recent weakness is an opportunity to accumulate. Despite risks such as inflation, labor, and supply chain issues, General Dynamics has adapted through major crises and is expected to benefit from a generational boom in defense spending.

title: 7 Undervalued S&P 500 Stocks to Buy in June
text: When attempting to build a portfolio of reliable entities, you probably can't go wrong with undervalued S&P 500 stocks. Featuring the market performance of 500 of the largest companies listed on stock exchanges in the U.S., you're dealing with proven winners.

title: General Dynamics (GD) Arm Wins $11.8M Deal for Knifefish UUV
text: General Dynamics' (GD) business unit clinches an $11.8-million modification contract involving Knifefish Block 0 to Block 1 retrofit performance.

title: U.S. Wide-Moat Stocks On Sale - The June 2023 Heat Map
text: Our 3-step process focuses on wide-moat stocks (as per Morningstar's rating). We are only interested in those targets that are attractively valued in historical comparison. We share the heat map of the most investable candidates that may be worth your time for further analysis.

title: General Dynamics: A Dividend Aristocrat With Upside Potential
text: General Dynamics is a Dividend Aristocrat having raised its dividend for 28 consecutive years. General Dynamics' most recent quarter was modestly better than the comparable quarter last year. The $33 billion worth of military equipment supplied to Ukraine by the US will have to be replaced at some point.

title: US debt ceiling deal strands $16 bln of defense side-projects
text: A $16 billion list of lower-priority defense items like tanks, helicopter upgrades and a ship, that would normally be paid for as part of the defense budget, could go unfunded after the U.S. passed a landmark bill that lifts the debt ceiling but curbs federal spending.
